examples: Corynebacterium glutamicum high-serine is used as a lysine-deficient strain of the bacteria fermentation in the industry.
Inhibits also most of other mammalian serine proteases but at a lower rate.
This lectin contains large amount of glutamic acid, aspartate, arginine and serine.
Dr. Snyder may have found one in his serendipitous discovery of how D-serine functions.
D-serine was of the "right-handed" configuration, which is almost never seen in the human body.
